title: Persona (single catalog / library entry)
description: The ONE uniform schema for a single persona, shared by all three sources: the curated catalog (`references/personas/<name>.json`), user personas (`~/.claude/dag/personas/*.json`), and project personas (`.dag/personas/*.json`). Required `name`, `role`, `description` (non-empty strings); optional `mandate`, `optimizes_for`, `skeptical_of`, `phase`, `pair_with` (strings), `qualifications` and `tags` (string arrays). `additionalProperties:false`. All optional fields are backward-compatible: a lean persona carrying only the three required fields still validates. Phase 1 discovers and merges files from all three sources into the candidate pool (override order: project > user > curated). This validates persona INPUT and is deliberately NOT a run artifact: it is meta-validated by `validate_run.py --self-check` but never required by a run (not in TOP_ARTIFACTS). Distinct from personas.schema.json, which validates the confirmed-roster OUTPUT sidecar. See templates/persona.json for a conforming example and references/personas/GUIDE.md for the discovery/merge conventions.

name.description: Concrete persona name, e.g. 'Java Backend Architect' — rename generic archetypes to be task-concrete.

description.description: What this persona is and the lens it brings.

mandate.description: Optional. The decision this persona owns.

optimizes_for.description: Optional. What this persona optimizes for.

skeptical_of.description: Optional. What this persona is skeptical of.

phase.description: Optional. The typical Dag phase/unit at which this persona is invoked, verbatim or faithfully trimmed from the catalog's Phase field (e.g. '6', '3/6').

qualifications.description: Optional. Each element carries one distinct qualification claim (and its cited source) from the catalog's Qualifications block. Omit entirely for lean personas that have no Qualifications block.

pair_with.description: Optional. The critic/verifier persona this entry names as its productive counterpart, when the source entry names a pairing.
